evals.prompt: Walk me through how to introduce QA into sprint planning for a team that currently has QA testing only after development is complete
anti_patterns: QA should only test after code is written
no mention of identifying gaps before development

evals.prompt: Design a Three Amigos session for a payment feature that touches auth, database, and a third-party payment processor
anti_patterns: product only OR dev only without cross-functional discussion

evals.prompt: Create a Definition of Done template for a team moving from test-after to shift-left practices
expected_patterns: unit tests OR business logic tests
anti_patterns: coverage threshold mentioned without baseline context
DoD items without clear pass/fail criteria

evals.prompt: A developer wrote a PR that fixes a bug with no test. How should QA review this using the shift-left PR checklist?
expected_patterns: regression test OR failing test first
test quality check OR meaningful assertions
anti_patterns: approve the PR without a test

evals.prompt: Explain when to use TDD vs. test-after using the decision guide. Give three scenarios where each approach is best.
expected_patterns: TDD for pure business logic OR validators OR calculators
TDD for bug fix with known reproduction
TDD for API endpoints with clear contract
test-after for exploratory UI OR prototyping
anti_patterns: no explanation of why each scenario fits its approach

evals.prompt: Design a dev/QA pairing session to create test cases for a coupon feature before any code is written
expected_patterns: test signatures OR test function names
unit tests AND integration tests AND e2e tests
anti_patterns: developer writes code first then QA tests
test names describe implementation not behavior

evals.prompt: Assess a team using the shift-left maturity model. They have QA reviews on high-risk PRs, some automated tests, and a DoD that mentions testing. What level are they at, and what's the next step?
expected_patterns: Three Amigos OR QA in sprint planning
measurement metric OR defects found earlier
anti_patterns: no measurement or success metric suggested
next step is more of the same

evals.prompt: A team wants to implement shift-left quickly by forcing TDD on all code and making Three Amigos mandatory for every story. What risks or anti-patterns does this approach have?
expected_patterns: All TDD All the Time OR anti-pattern
quality gates without buy-in OR anti-pattern
overwhelm OR start small prove value
anti_patterns: teams should do all practices at once
no mention of team resistance or sustainability

evals.prompt: Write test cases for a password validator using Red-Green-Refactor. Show the first RED test, GREEN implementation, then REFACTOR
expected_patterns: minimum code to pass OR GREEN

evals.prompt: Review this PR checklist item for an AI-generated feature. What additional checks are needed beyond standard testability?
expected_patterns: AI provenance disclosed OR agent/model named
tests written first OR by human
prompt and model version pinned OR AI Configs
feature flag OR runtime kill switch
prompt eval test OR regression test
no fabricated APIs OR imports verified
anti_patterns: no special handling for AI-generated code
tests are AI-generated without human review
no kill switch for AI feature
